extracted_codes.context: The PhEMA algorithm selected cases only, who were defined as those in the study population with any BPH-related ICD-9 codes on 2 separate days
Within the PhEMA algorithm, the exclusion used just ICD-9 billing codes, although the original algorithm also used ICD-O-3 codes from tumor registries
The PhEMA algorithm used a combination of standard medical terminology codes for medications (RxNorm and NDC)
The PhEMA algorithm used a combination of standard medical terminology codes for medications (RxNorm and NDC), diagnostic codes (ICD-9), and procedure codes (CPT)

clinical_criteria: BPH-related ICD-9 codes on 2 separate days
At least one BPH medication or BPH-related surgery code
No evidence of prostate, penile, urethral, or bladder cancer
algorithm_summary: The PhEMA BPH algorithm identifies cases of benign prostatic hyperplasia by selecting males age 40 and older who have no evidence of prostate, penile, urethral, or bladder cancer in their EHR. Cases are defined as those with BPH-related ICD-9 diagnostic codes on 2 separate days, plus at least one BPH medication (RxNorm/NDC codes) or BPH-related surgery code (CPT). The algorithm excludes cancer patients using ICD-9 billing codes and was designed to be executable across multiple EHR systems using standardized computable formats (HQMF/QDM). The algorithm demonstrates >90% positive predictive value and >90% overlap with original eMERGE implementations at most sites.
